data.title: Airbus’s yearly deliveries rise 4% to 766 commercial planes
data.description:
Airbus’s (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) deliveries of commercial planes rose 4.2% from a year earlier to 766 in 2024, the highest in five years. The delivery total likely affirms that the European aviation company maintained its spot as the world’s biggest maker of jetliners, as U.S. rival Boeing (NYSE:BA) struggles to overcome safety issues and supply-chain constraints.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY) (OTCPK:EADSF) had set a yearly target of about 770 jet deliveries amid its own efforts to overcome shortages in labor and parts.

The company received 878 gross orders and a net total of 826 after cancellations, down 61% from a record 2023.

Boeing (NYSE:BA) had 370 net orders after cancellations by the end of November. The company is due to report deliveries on Tuesday.

The Federal Aviation Administration last year capped Boeing’s (BA) output of its best-selling 737 Max because of safety concerns. A 737 Max 9 model flown by Alaska Airlines lost a door panel shortly after takeoff, forcing an emergency landing in Portland, Oregon. Boeing's (BA) output also was hampered by a seven-week strike by machinists.

data.title: Liberty Media appoints former NBA China top boss Derek Chang as CEO
data.description:
Liberty Media (NASDAQ:FWONA) has named Derek Chang president and chief executive officer, the company said on Wednesday after the bell.

Chang has been a director of Liberty Media since March 2021. He was also the CEO of NBA China from 2018 to 2020.

Most recently, Chang served as the executive chairman of EverPass Media, a joint venture he cofounded with Alex Kaplan in partnership with the NFL and RedBird Capital Partners.

Chang will start in his new role on February 1, 2025. Chairman John Malone will serve as interim CEO until that time.

Chang will also join the executive committee of the board with John Malone, vice chairman Dob Bennett, and Chase Carey.

Stefano Domenicali, president & CEO of Formula 1, will report to a committee of the Liberty Media board comprised of Bennett, Carey, and Chang.

data.title: Voya sees Q4 alternative investment income, prepayment fees lagging long-term expectations
data.description:
Voya Financial (NYSE:VOYA) estimated Q4 alternative investment income and prepayment fees of ~$40M-$48M before taxes, about $8M-$16M below its long-term expectations, before variable and incentive compensation, the company said Thursday.

The long-term expectation for alternative investments is a 9% annual return, and for prepayment fees is a 10-basis-point annual contribution yield.

The results are preliminary, as the company hasn't yet completed financial closing procedures for Q4. In addition, its independent registered public accounting firm hasn't yet audited, reviewed, or performed any procedures regarding its preliminary Q4 results.

data.title: Bonds halt selloff ahead of key jobs report; Stock market shut for Carter's mourning
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields fell on Thursday in a truncated session, as a bond selloff finally eased up. Meanwhile, stock index futures slipped, continuing Wall Street's seesaw start to 2025.

The stock market is closed for a national day of mourning for former U.S. President Jimmy Carter. The bond market shut early at 1400 ET.

Benchmark S&P 500 futures (SPX) ended -0.20%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.13%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.28%.

The U.S. 10-year yield (US10Y) fell 1 bais point to 4.69%. The instrument hit levels not seen since November 2023 on Wednesday. The shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) shed 2 basis points to 4.27%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

"We expect trading activity to be light and liquidity to weaken (today), given U.S. equity markets are closed and U.S. bond markets close early for former President Carter’s funeral, and that the December employment report looms on Friday," JPMorgan's Jay Barry, Jason Hunter, and Phoebe White said on Wednesday.

U.S. equities whipsawed through a volatile day in the previous session, eventually ending mixed. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) added +0.16% to finish at 5,918.23 points, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) climbed +0.25% to settle at 42,635.20 points. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slipped -0.06% to conclude at 19,478.88 points.

Concerns over the effects of incoming President Donald Trump's policies on inflation, along with a Federal Reserve signaling a slower pace of interest rate cuts has pressured sentiment in the fledgling new year.

The minutes of the Fed's December monetary policy committee meeting published yesterday confirmed that committee participants believed they were "at or near the point at which it would be appropriate to slow the pace of policy easing." The minutes also showed that there was "elevated uncertainty" among committee members "regarding specifics about the scope and timing of potential changes to trade, immigration, fiscal, and regulatory policies and their potential effects on the economy."

"The minutes to the December FOMC meeting provided little new information on the near-term path of policy, indicating that the decision to cut last month was 'finely balanced,'" Barry, Hunter, and White said.

"The minutes clarified that expected tariffs were one reason the staff revised their inflation forecast higher. Meanwhile, there was no discussion about the forward path of balance sheet policy, although it was noted that the average estimate of survey respondents for the timing of the end of balance sheet runoff shifted a bit later, to June 2025," they added.

Market participants will return on Friday with their attention squarely focused on the December U.S. nonfarm payrolls report. Data on the labor market has pointed to renewed resilience recently after significant cooling in the middle of last year, which has led to traders dialing back their Fed rate cut bets.

data.title: Musk, attorneys, ask judges to auction off OpenAI stake: report
data.description:
Elon Musk and his attorneys have asked judges in the states of California and Delaware to cause an auction that would force OpenAI to sell off a portion of its business, the Financial Times reported.

Musk attorney Marc Toberoff wrote a letter to the law officers of each state on behalf of investors who would want to own a part of OpenAI, the generative artificial intelligence startup backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), the news outlet added, citing the letter.

OpenAI has no plans for an auction and that Musk and his team "want more chaos," a source told the news outlet.

OpenAI and Musk did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Musk's messy history with OpenAI

Musk and his lawyers filed an injunction in November 2024 in an effort to stop OpenAI's conversion to a for-profit structure. The injunction also attempted to prevent OpenAI from engaging in what Musk's lawyers claimed was anticompetitive behavior.

OpenAI was founded in 2015 by Musk, current Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman and several others. By the summer of 2017, Musk "agreed that a for-profit was the next step for OpenAI to advance the mission," OpenAI said in a post on its website last month.

By that fall, Musk "demanded majority equity" in the company, along with what OpenAI called "absolute control" and the requirement that he be named Chief Executive in the for-profit company.

That September, Musk created the public benefit corporation called Open Artificial Intelligence Technologies, Inc.

Following the creation of the corporation, OpenAI said it rejected Elon's terms "because giving him unilateral control of OpenAI and its technology would be contrary to the mission."

A few months later, in March 2018, Musk resigned as co-chair of OpenAI.

OpenAI's value

OpenAI, creator of the popular ChatGPT chatbot, has raised billions of dollars in funding in recent memory, including a $6.6B funding round that values the company at $157B. Microsoft has invested approximately $14B into OpenAI, including joining the most recent funding.

Musk, who heads up OpenAI competitor xAI, filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for allegedly putting "profit over humanity." (xAI recently raised funding that valued the company at $50B and just unveiled a standalone app for its Grok chatbot on iOS.)

He dropped the initial lawsuit, but re-opened it this past August on the basis that the original lawsuit "lacked teeth."

Musk's lawsuit also recently added Microsoft, LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman and Dee Templeton, a current Microsoft executive who previously worked at OpenAI, as co-defendants.

data.title: Federal Trade Commission sets closed door meeting for next Thursday
data.description:
The Federal Trade Commission has scheduled a closed-door meeting for next Thursday as FTC Chair Lina Khan's reign nears an end.

The meeting, for the consideration of multiple nonpublic law enforcement matters, is scheduled for 1 pm on Jan. 16, according to a listing for the event on the FTC's website.

Investors often speculate when the meeting notices are given that the FTC may be voting on an ongoing deal that's being reviewed. While sometimes these meetings are about public companies, oftentimes they are on private firms or other matters. Potential deals that the FTC may be looking at include IBM's (IBM) purchase of HashiCorp (HCP), CoStar's (CSGP) acquisition of Matterport (MTTR), Surmodics (SRDX) sale to private equity firm GTCR or possibly Boeing's (BA) planned purchase of Spirit Aerosystems (SPR).

President-elect Donald Trump last month picked Andrew Ferguson to be the next chair of the FTC, replacing Khan.

data.title: Wiz names Merchant CFO as it nears IPO following busy 2024
data.description:
Wiz announced the appointment of Fazal Merchant as its new chief financial officer and president on Thursday as the cybersecurity startup expands its executive team and makes moves for an initial public offering within a year or so.

Merchant previously served as co-CEO of Tanium, a privately held cybersecurity firm located in Kirkland, Wash. He was also CFO at DreamWorks Animation.

The last year was busy for Wiz, a cloud security company headquartered in New York City with offices in Tel Aviv. In 2024, the company acquired Gem Security, a cloud detection and response startup, and Dazz, a security and risk management startup, for a combined $800M. The company also raised $1B in a funding round.

In July, Wiz walked away from a $23B acquisition deal with Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) and refocused its efforts on going public.

"As CFO & President, he will up-level our business with strategies and processes to help us mature and seize the opportunity that lies before us," said Wiz CEO and co-founder Assaf Rappaport, in a blog post. "With Fazal on board, the team will be better positioned than ever to navigate the road ahead while continuing to innovate on behalf of our customers."

data.title: After Santa Rally letdown, January advance 'key' to positive 2025
data.description:
Santa Claus delivered a loss during the “Santa Rally” period ushering in a new equity-trading year, but history shows a winning January can lay the groundwork for an S&P 500 (SP500) annual gain, the publisher of the Stock Trader's Almanac said.

The Santa Rally period produced a 0.5% decline from the end of trade on Dec. 23, 2024, through Jan. 3’s close. Now, investors are putting the so-called "January Barometer" in view. After seven Santa Rally failures going back to 1967, the S&P 500 (SP500) finished with positive annual returns in six of those following years after January gains.

18.2% was the average return in those seven years. The table below comes from AlmanacTrader posting on X (formerly Twitter):

The “January Barometer has got to be positive,” to set the stage for a 2025 advance, Jeffrey Hirsch, editor-in-chief of the Stock Trader's Almanac, told Fox Business this week. He noted his father and the publication’s creator, Yale Hirsch, created the Santa Rally and January Barometer indicators in 1972.

“It’s really all about January being the key here,” Hirsch said, adding the market this month will see the Trump administration coming into the White House. “So we’ll have a good read on what's going on in D.C., we'll have a better read on inflation in the coming weeks when some data comes out.”

The S&P 500 (SP500) can be tracked through ETFs including: (VOO), (SPY), (SDS), (UPRO), and (IVV).

Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) exchange-traded funds include: (QQQ), (TQQQ), (SQQQ), (DIA), (UDOW) and (DOG).

data.title: Apartment Investment Management expands review of options
data.description:
Apartment Investment Management's (NYSE:AIV) board will explore additional alternatives to further unlock shareholder value after it agreed to sell its Brickell Bay properties for ~$520M, the company known as Aimco said on Thursday.

The expanded review will consider such options as reducing exposure to development activity and monetizing certain assets. It may also include exploring the potential sales of the major parts of its business, an acceleration of individual asset sales, or a sale or merger of the company as a whole.

"Our guiding principle will be to produce an outcome that delivers maximum value to Aimco shareholders," the company said in a statement.

Aimco's (NYSE:AIV) investment committee, comprised of the board's four independent directors, is overseeing the process. Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC is serving as the company's financial adviser.

The company can't assure that that expanded strategic review will result in any transaction or other strategic changes.

In the past year, Aimco (AIV) shares rose 9.4%, lagging the S&P 500's 24% growth.

data.title: Bayer hot flashes therapy for breast cancer patients hits main goal in late-stage trial
data.description:
Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) (OTCPK:BAYRY) announced Thursday that elinzanetant, its experimental therapy for hot flashes, reached the main goals in a late-stage trial for women with or at a high risk of developing breast cancer.

Hot flashes represent a common adverse effect of endocrine therapy, a well-established adjuvant therapy for hormone-positive breast cancer.

Announcing topline data from its OASIS 4 Phase 3 study, the German conglomerate said that its non-hormonal treatment option compared to placebo cut the occurrence of hot flashes (vasomotor symptoms) with a statistically significant effect from Week 4 to Week 12.

OASIS 4, the fourth trial in the company's OASIS program to generate positive topline data, was designed to evaluate elinzanetant in treating hot flashes caused by endocrine therapy in women with or at a high risk of developing hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer.

Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) noted that the global trial of 474 patients also reached all secondary endpoints, including a measure related to the reductions in the severity of VMS, and added that the drug's safety profile over 52 weeks was consistent with findings from the prior trials.

"The positive results from OASIS 4 bring us one step closer to a much-needed non-hormonal option for managing VMS in breast cancer patients and women at risk of breast cancer," said Dr. Fatima Cardoso, OASIS 4's lead investigator.

Multiple regulatory submissions for elinzanetant are currently underway in the U.S. and other countries based on the company's OASIS program.
